title: Duo keys should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don't need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Duo Security Integration Key detected (DI prefix). Duo integration keys identify your Duo application and are used together with the secret key to authenticate API requests. Exposing an integration key combined with its secret key allows attackers to bypass multi-factor authentication controls, enumerate enrolled users, and potentially disable 2FA for accounts. Revoke and regenerate credentials immediately in the Duo Admin Panel.

Duo Security Secret Key detected in variable assignment. Duo secret keys are used to sign API requests to Duo's services for multi-factor authentication. Combined with an integration key, exposing this secret allows attackers to forge API requests, bypass MFA, access enrolled user lists, and potentially disable two-factor authentication for accounts protected by Duo. Reset immediately in the Duo Admin Panel.

Duo Security API hostname detected. While API hostnames alone are not credentials, they uniquely identify your Duo account and when combined with integration/secret keys found nearby in code form complete Duo credentials. This finding indicates Duo credentials may be hardcoded in the vicinity. Review surrounding code for hardcoded integration keys and secret keys.
